Staff Guide

Skyla's Diabetes Care Plan

Everything you need to support Skyla — her insulin system, meals, hypos, and what to do if something doesn't look right

How It Works Hypos Highs Time to Eat? History Adding Notes Watch FAQs Contacts
🔄

How the Closed-Loop System Works

Three components working together

Skyla's diabetes is managed by a closed-loop system (Android APS). The glucose sensor reads her blood sugar every 5 minutes, sends it to the Android APS app on her phone, which then automatically tells her insulin pump how much insulin to give.

You don't need to adjust the automatic insulin. Your main job is to tell the app about carbohydrates when Skyla eats.

Dexcom G7 Sensor

Worn on Skyla's upper arm. Reads blood glucose every 5 minutes via Bluetooth to her phone. Changed every 10 days.

Android APS App

On Skyla's phone (passcode: 1013). Receives sensor data and decides insulin doses. Needs carb information from you at mealtimes.

Omnipod Dash

Small device on Skyla's tummy. Delivers insulin automatically via a thin tube. Do not remove it.

⚠️ Important: Sensor readings are approximately 10–15 minutes behind a finger-prick reading. If blood glucose is rising or dropping rapidly, the actual value may be several points further along than shown.

The Android APS App — Home Screen

💡 You don't need to understand everything on this screen. The app runs automatically in the background — the key things to know are covered in this guide. The table below is just for reference.
  • Large number Current blood glucose (mmol/L). Green = in range, yellow/red = outside range.
  • Arrow Direction of travel. → stable, ↗ rising, ↘ falling, ⇊ falling fast.
  • Δ values Change in last 5, 15, and 40 minutes.
  • Syringe icon Insulin on board (IOB) — active insulin still working.
  • Wheat icon Carbs on board (COB) — carbs still being absorbed.
  • T:0% Temporary basal rate. T:0% means pump is paused (e.g. during exercise).
  • Graph Blood glucose over time. Green zone = target range (4–10). Dotted line = prediction.
  • Bottom buttons INSULIN (manual dose), CARBS (enter carbs only), CALCULATOR (use this for meals).
📲 Phone passcode: 1013. Skyla's phone must stay within 6 feet of her at all times so the sensor can communicate with the app.
🍬

Managing Hypos

Treating or preventing hypos is the most important thing you can do for Skyla.

Skyla's target blood glucose range is 4.0–10.0 mmol/L. A hypo occurs when her blood glucose drops below 4.0 mmol/L, or when it looks like it is heading there.

📈 Recognising a Hypo
Watch for a reading below 4.0 on the app or watch, or trend arrows pointing sharply downward. Double down arrows (⇊) mean she is dropping fast — act before she reaches 4.0, don't wait for the number to get there.

Treatment Steps

⚠ Double down arrows (⇊) on the app or watch mean she is dropping fast — act before she reaches 4.0, don’t wait for the number to get there.

💡 What to expect after treatment
Her BG may spike above the normal range after a hypo treatment — this is normal. Android APS will work to bring it back down gradually. Keep an eye on it but do not give extra insulin manually unless advised.

Manual Finger Prick Check

If you are ever unsure about the reading shown on the phone or watch, double-check with a finger prick. The device is a Freestyle Optium Neo — it is kept in Skyla's bumbag along with the test strips.

  • Insert the correct test strip — blue strip for blood glucose, purple strip for ketones
  • Prick the side of a finger (less painful than the pad)
  • Touch the blood to the strip — a result appears within a few seconds
👍 Skyla knows this process well — your job is simply to support and reassure her.
📈

Treating High Blood Sugar

If Skyla's blood sugar reaches 14.0 mmol/L or above, a ketone test should be done using the purple strip in the Freestyle Optium Neo (in her bumbag).

  1. Confirm blood sugar is 14.0 or above on the watch or phone
  2. Do a ketone test using the purple strip in the Freestyle Optium Neo
  3. If ketones are 0.6 or higher — call parents immediately
  4. If ketones are below 0.6 — watch and wait
  5. If still above 14.0 after 30 minutes, do another ketone test and repeat
Android APS should auto-correct high blood sugar on its own. Do not give extra insulin manually. Call parents if you are concerned or if ketones are 0.6 or above.
🌾

Time to Eat?

When Skyla eats, you must enter the carb amount into the Android APS Calculator. The app will calculate the right insulin dose and send it to the pump automatically.

1 From the phone home screen, tap the Android APS widget (shows blood glucose) or open the Android APS app.
2 Tap the CALCULATOR button (bottom right). Enter the carb amount using the + next to Carbs. Change carb time to 15 mins, then tap OK.
3 A confirmation screen shows the calculated insulin dose and carbs. Tap OK to confirm delivery.
🤔 What if Skyla doesn't eat?
If Skyla doesn't eat the food you have given insulin for, she may start to drop. Consider giving her a biscuit (5–8g carbs) or a banana (15–20g carbs) to cover the insulin and prevent a hypo. Keep an eye on her BG and treat as normal if she goes below 4.0.

Lunch Boxes

Skyla's parents will send her in with a balanced lunch box, including some free foods (foods with negligible carbs) such as Babybels and Peperami — these do not need to be counted.

A note inside the lunch box will list all the foods that contain carbs and the amount, for example:

  • Sandwich — 18g
  • Rice cake — 5g
  • Grapes — 12g

Ask Skyla what she would like to eat, then add up the carbs for those items (e.g. 18 + 5 + 12 = 35g). Enter that total into the Android APS Calculator on her phone.

Check that the insulin has been administered — you should see it on the graph or in the Treatments tab.

Wait 10–15 minutes before she starts eating.

📱 Carbs & Cals App

Carbs & Cals is a free app (with optional paid upgrade) available for both Android and Apple. It can be used to look up and approximate the carb content of different foods using photos and portion sizes.

Download it here: carbsandcals.com/product/app

Ideally, weigh the food Skyla is going to eat and use the app to work out the carb content as accurately as possible.

In an ideal world, weigh the food and enter the bolus 5–10 minutes before she eats. In practice, entering upfront and topping up afterwards works well. Always keep a banana on standby in case she doesn't eat her meal after a big bolus.

🍝 Pasta — Special Rule
Pasta releases carbohydrates slowly over 2–4 hours. Giving the full bolus upfront will likely cause a hypo. Split the bolus in two: give the first half upfront, and the second half after around 90–120 minutes. For example: 30g at 12:00, then 20g at 13:30.
📋

History

If you want to review what insulin and carbs have been given, you can check the Treatments log.

1 On the home screen, tap the three dots (⋮) in the top-right corner.
2 Tap Treatments from the menu.
3 The Treatments screen shows a full record of all carbs entered (in orange/green) and insulin delivered (in white/blue), with timestamps.
📝

Adding Notes

Use NOTES to keep a record of important events that affect Skyla's blood sugar. Parents can see exactly what happened and when.

👩‍🏫 Most Common School Uses
  • 🍬 Low blood sugar treatments: “1 tablet”
  • 📈 Recording ketones: “Ketones: 0.2”
  • 😰 If Skyla feels unwell: “Feeling tired. Checked BG”
  • ⚠️ Unusual situations: “Forgot lunch insulin, gave at 1:30pm”

Step-by-Step Instructions

1 Tap the ACTIONS tab at the top of the screen.
2 Tap the NOTE icon.
3 Type your note in the Notes box. ⚠️ Leave all other boxes empty.
4 Tap OK, then tap OK again to confirm.
5 ✅ Your note now appears on the graph. The app records the exact time automatically.

Quick Reference

Situation Example Note
🍬 Hypo treatment “1 tab”
😰 Ketones checked “Ketones: 0.2”
🍰 Extra food “Birthday cake”
⏰ Timing issues “Missed pre-bolus”
🚨 Emergency “Called parents 2:15pm”
📏 Keep it short — just the key facts
Don't worry about perfect spelling, long explanations, or medical terminology. Only note the unusual stuff — normal meals and routine activities don't need a note.
⌚

The Watch

Skyla will bring a watch for the supervising adult to wear. It shows her blood glucose at a glance without needing to check the phone.

Display elementWhat it means
Large number (top)Current blood glucose in mmol/L. Target range: 4.0–10.0.
Arrows (top right)Trend direction. ↓↓ = falling fast and may need treatment soon.
Small number (top left)Minutes since last reading. Should update every 5 min. If over 15 min, check Skyla has her phone nearby.
Change value (e.g. −1.1)Change in blood glucose since last reading.
GraphBlood glucose over the last few hours. Red line = hypo threshold (4.0). Yellow line = upper target.
❓

Troubleshooting & FAQs

Common Issues & Quick Solutions

Problem What to do
⌚ Watch not updating
  1. Check Skyla has her phone nearby
  2. Make sure phone Bluetooth is ON
  3. Restart Skyla's phone
  4. If still not working — ☎️ call parents
💉 Unsure if insulin was delivered
  1. ⏳ Wait 5 minutes
  2. Check Treatment History
  3. ❌ Do NOT give insulin again
  4. ☎️ Call parents if unsure
🟡 Yellow warning triangle ✅ Usually fixes itself — keep monitoring, system still works
🔴 Red warning triangle 🚨 Call parents — this indicates a system problem
🔊 Pump keeps beeping ☎️ Call parents
🚨 Red Flag Situations — Call Parents NOW
  • Blood sugar below 3.5 for 30 minutes AND won't come up after treatment
  • Blood sugar above 14.0 for more than 1 hour — see High Blood Sugar
  • Any red warning triangles on phone
  • Pump won't stop beeping or alarming
  • Skyla feels very unwell (vomiting, severe headache, etc.)
  • Phone completely dead and won't charge
🟡 When NOT to Panic — These Usually Fix Themselves
  • Yellow warning triangles
  • Sensor readings slightly delayed (normal lag time)
  • Phone app looks different than usual
  • Watch shows old reading (if phone is nearby)
🎯 Remember the Basics

Your main job:

  • Give hypo treatment when BG is below 4.0
  • Help with meals when needed
  • Call for help when unsure

🚫 Don't try to:

  • Fix technical problems yourself
  • Give extra insulin without checking
  • Ignore warning signs
📞
